name: Cinnamon Rolls
method: In a large mixing bowl, combine the warm water, active dry yeast, and granulated sugar. Let it steep for 10 minutes.
Stir in the butter, milk, salt, and remaining granulated sugar.
Stir in the flour. Using your hands, knead a dough from the mixture.
Place the kneaded dough back in the bowl. Cover with kitchen cloth and let the dough rise at room temperature for 90 minutes.
Meanwhile, in a small mixing bowl, combine the brown sugar and ground cinnamon.
Roll out the risen dough into a rectangular shape ¼-inch thick. Spread the butter on top and sprinkle with the cinnamon mixture.
Roll out the dough into a roulade. Cut the roulade into 1-inch-thick slices.
Arrange the cinnamon rolls on a baking sheet lined with a piece of parchment paper. Bake for 30-35 minutes or until golden-brown in color.
To make the glaze, in a small mixing bowl, combine the powdered sugar, milk, and vanilla extract. Spread the glaze on top of the baked cinnamon rolls.
